title: [aurorus] Battle Guide
summary: A [rock]/[ice] [snow-warning] lead whose [freeze-dry] punishes [water] cores other [ice] attackers can't touch, while setting up the whole snow plan.
strengths: 123 HP absorbs a neutral special hit for a free utility turn
[freeze-dry] reads [water] types as 2x, an angle no other [ice] move provides
[snow-warning] auto-starts Snow for slush-rush and aurora-veil partners
weaknesses: 4x Fighting and 4x Steel — close-combat or iron-head is an instant OHKO
Base 58 Speed leaves it underneath essentially every offensive threat
72 physical Def cannot absorb any unresisted physical attack

recommendedMoves.reason: Mandatory coverage for Steel and Fire switch-ins
Reliable [rock] STAB for Flying and Bug punishment
Lead utility that multiplies the team's KO ceiling

recommendedItems.reason: Compensates for the 58 Speed so guarantees one utility turn
Turns 123 HP into a long-game recovery pool

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[aurorus] is a huge-bodied [rock]/[ice] special attacker whose value in Champions MA comes from two specific things: the hidden ability [snow-warning] auto-starts Snow on entry, and [freeze-dry] reads [water]-types as 2x damage. That combination gives it an offensive angle that no other [ice] special attacker in the pool can reproduce, and it slots cleanly as a lead/utility piece for any Snow-based team build.

## Strengths
[freeze-dry] is the headline. Most [ice] attackers are walled by [water] cores; [aurorus] hits them for 2x and uses 99 SpA to actually capitalize. Stealth-[rock] on the same set means the first turn contributes both an entry hazard and a hard damage click, and [snow-warning] immediately unlocks aurora-veil, slush-rush, and [ice]-body payoffs for the rest of the team. The 123 HP pool is enormous — with investment it can absorb a single neutral special attack and continue working, which is plenty for a lead role.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Fighting and Steel both hit for 4x. A single close-combat or iron-head is a guaranteed OHKO, and Ground, [water], and [rock] all land for 2x. Base 58 Speed means [aurorus] loses the Speed race to every offensive threat that matters, so it essentially cannot safely switch in on a live attacker — it has to come in on a predicted swap. The 72 physical Def is also fragile enough that even uninvested physical attackers can punch through it. The honest answer is that [aurorus] is a one-to-two-turn utility piece that leaves the field by design, not a wincon that wants to stick around.

## Recommended Moveset
[freeze-dry] is the locked STAB main specifically for its 2x [water] matchup. [earth-power] fills the coverage slot, answering Steel and Fire switch-ins that would otherwise wall the set. [ancient-power] is the reliable [rock] STAB for Flying and Bug targets (power-gem isn't in the legal learnset here). The last slot is [stealth-rock], which is what justifies [aurorus] as a lead over other [ice] attackers — one turn of setup multiplies every follow-up attacker's KO potential. If the team already carries a rocks setter, blizzard is the alternative damage slot.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default — with 58 Speed and a stacked weakness list, the sash guarantees at least one utility turn. [leftovers] is the long-game alternative for teams that want [aurorus] to linger in back as a passive damage sponge, leveraging the 123 HP pool. The ability must be [snow-warning] — refrigerate is an offensive gimmick that only works on Normal moves [aurorus] doesn't run. Tera [ice] maxes [freeze-dry] damage for a one-shot window, and Tera [water] flips the 4x Fighting matchup by turning close-combat into a resisted hit.

## Teammates & Synergy
An aurora-veil setter (Alolan Ninetales if available; otherwise a dedicated screen user) is the primary partner — [aurorus] starts the Snow, the setter puts up the screen, and the rest of the team sweeps behind the veil. A Fighting-resist pivot (Psychic or Fairy ally) is mandatory to cover the 4x weakness, and a slush-rush user scales directly off the Snow that [aurorus] turns on. Because [aurorus] already brings [stealth-rock], don't double-stack another rocks setter.

## Play Patterns
Lead [aurorus], turn one drop [stealth-rock] while Snow starts automatically, turn two either swap out or click [freeze-dry] into a predicted [water] switch-in. Do not try to stay in on any live Fighting or Steel attacker; eat the first turn of damage only if it's special. Save Tera [water] for the moment a close-combat is telegraphed and either survive the flip or pivot on the resisted hit. [aurorus] is a two-turn card that leaves the field by design — stretching it further is almost always a losing trade.
